body.title: CLOSED: Delete reconsolidation Path B — confirm_recall, contradict_recall, hook_search_results
body.result: Deleted Path B: reconsolidation.py (229 lines) + test file (316 lines) + cortex hook. Two flag keys → one. commit daf0bce4
body.description:
Two parallel reconsolidation mechanisms exist with different flag keys and zero crosstalk. Path A (cortex._flag_for_reconsolidation → metadata.reconsolidate_pending → NE._reconsolidation_pass) is live and wired. Path B (reconsolidation.hook_search_results → _recall_pending dict → confirm_recall/contradict_recall → metadata.reconsolidation_flag) has no consumers — the dict grows forever under long runs (memory leak). Delete Path B.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/memory/reconsolidation.py (remove confirm_recall, contradict_recall, hook_search_results), wild_igor/igor/memory/cortex.py (remove hook_search_results call at ~line 2780)
**Design rules:** none-apply (pure deletion)
**Scope boundary:** IN: delete Path B consumer functions + cortex hook call. OUT: do not touch Path A (_flag_for_reconsolidation, NE._reconsolidation_pass, reconsolidate_pending key).
**Test plan:** remove tests that exercise confirm_recall/contradict_recall/hook_search_results from tests/test_reconsolidation.py; verify test_d126_postgres still passes.
**Pass condition:** pytest passes; grep for hook_search_results in cortex.py returns nothing; _recall_pending dict is gone.
